name: Around Clermont-le-Fort
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Auragne are situated in the Haute-Garonne department of Occitanie, offering access to diverse cycling terrains. The region is characterized by flat, well-maintained paths along the Canal des Deux Mers and the Garonne River, providing relaxed rides through rural countryside. For more challenging routes, the area serves as a gateway to the foothills of the Pyrenees, featuring varied elevation. Forests and open landscapes provide tranquil settings for exploring local flora and fauna. Best touring cycling routes around Auragne The most popular touring cycling route is Around Clermont-le-Fort, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alley and church of Lézat – View of Lake Carla-Bayle loop from Auterive, a difficult 58.9 miles (94.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views, including Lake Carla-Bayle. Local touring cyclists also love the Chemin de roudet – Church of Pinsaguel loop from Auterive, a 19.3 miles (31.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Auragne is defined by the Garonne River, the Canal des Deux Mers, and rolling rural countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy canal paths to more challenging routes with varied elevation. The routes in Auragne are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Auragne's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Auragne?
A: There are over 190 touring cycling routes in the Auragne area, catering to various skill levels and preferences. You'll find a wide selection, from leisurely rides along canals to more challenging routes with significant elevation.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Auragne?
A: The region offers diverse terrain. You can enjoy flat, well-maintained paths along the Canal des Deux Mers and the Garonne River, which are perfect for relaxed rides through rural countryside. For those seeking more challenge, the area also provides access to the foothills of the Pyrenees, featuring varied elevation and more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Auragne?
A: Yes, the flat, well-maintained towpaths along the Canal des Deux Mers are particularly suitable for families and cyclists of all skill levels. These routes are often shaded and largely inaccessible to cars, ensuring a secure and pleasant experience.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38672509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alley and church of Lézat – View of Lake Carla-Bayle loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Additionally, Auragne's proximity to the Pyrenees means you can access iconic passes like Col de Peyresourde and Col du Portillon for truly demanding rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Auragne?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in the Auragne area are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e929412575/around-clermont-le-fort" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Around Clermont-le-Fort&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6983058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chemin de roudet – Pesquies Windmill loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt;, which allow you to return to your starting point without retracing your path.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Auragne?
A: Along the routes, you can explore charming towns, vineyards, and historical sites. The region is known for its 'beautiful Toulouse houses,' castles, and churches. You might also encounter natural beauty along the Garonne River and through forests. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3091341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Notre-Dame des Bois Chapel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesquieu-Lauragais Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Auragne?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in July and August, but the shaded canal paths provide a cooler option. Winter can be cooler but still offers opportunities for cycling on clear days.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Auragne?
A: The touring cycling routes around Auragne are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the tranquil canal paths to the scenic rural landscapes, and the opportunities to discover local culture and natural beauty.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for?
A: Yes, beyond the general scenic beauty, you can visit specific landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin de Montbrun-Lauragais&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4021627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montgeard and its church&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7039225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrims' hostel near Ayguesvives&lt;/a&gt;. These offer interesting stops along your cycling journey.

Q: What kind of cycling routes are available for beginners?
A: Beginners will find many suitable options, particularly along the Canal des Deux Mers and the Garonne River, which offer generally flat and easy terrain. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/29555838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chemin de roudet – Church of Pinsaguel loop from Auterive&lt;/a&gt; are classified as easy and provide a gentle introduction to touring cycling in the region.

Q: Is it possible to access cycling routes near Auragne using public transport?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads in Auragne may be limited, the region is well-connected by train and bus services to larger towns like Toulouse and Auterive. From these hubs, you may be able to cycle to the starting points of various routes, especially those along the Canal du Midi or Garonne River which often pass through towns with public transport access.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Auragne?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for many routes, such as Auterive or Clermont-le-Fort. Look for designated public parking areas within these communities, especially near canal access points or local amenities.
